summary: End-to-end verification of AccessVerse (Phase 1) after backend fix (Body(...) instead of Field(...) on PATCH routes). Ran pytest suite for /api endpoints and Playwright UI flows through the external ingress URL. All 3 backend tests pass (root, quote lifecycle POST/GET/PATCH, consultation lifecycle POST/GET/PATCH). All UI flows verified: Get Free Quote modal + submission, Book Consultation modal + submission, Compliance Dashboard listing + PATCH status via native <select>, Before/After slider drag, Floating Accessibility Panel (contrast/text scaling/dyslexia/focus/screen-reader).

issues: The 'Get Free Quote' hero button is missing the data-testid={ACCESS_VERSE.quoteRequestBtn}. It is present in the top-right header CTA but not on the primary hero button (Home.jsx around line 217). Playwright succeeded via the top nav button, but the constant is defined for both. Consider adding data-testid to the hero variant for consistent QA.

action_items: Optional: Since the review request refers to '/dashboard' as a URL, but the app is a single-page (App.js only renders <Home/>) and Dashboard is accessed via the 'Compliance Tracker' tab (data-testid=nav-admin-dashboard-tab). Consider adding react-router so /dashboard directly loads the Dashboard view if that is desired.

Add data-testid=quote-request-btn to the hero 'Get Free Quote' button in Home.jsx (currently only the header CTA has it visually; hero button uses onClick={() => setIsQuoteOpen(true)} without a testid).

Consider gracefully degrading (or making non-blocking with asyncio.create_task) the send_notification_email call on POST /api/quotes and /api/consultations. Currently it is awaited before returning; if Resend proxy is ever slow the API response is blocked (~30s timeout). Response time was acceptable during testing (~1.5s create for quote, ~2s consult), but this is a scalability risk.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: send_notification_email is awaited inside POST handlers. If email delivery is slow, POST latency increases. Prefer asyncio.create_task(...) so the client gets a 201 immediately, since the review request explicitly says email 'should not block form success responses.'
server.py: Model QuoteResponse/ConsultationResponse manual construction duplicates fields already in doc_obj; consider .model_dump(exclude={'_id'}) or a helper to reduce duplication.
Dashboard.jsx uses native <select> for status — no data-testid on the select element; add one to make status-change automation deterministic.
App.js has no routing; the review request references /dashboard as a URL but that path silently falls back to Home. Either implement routing or update the requirement wording.
Home.jsx hero 'Get Free Quote' button omits data-testid (constant exists in accessverse.js but not applied).
test_credentials: N/A - endpoints and dashboard are public.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created 1 quote (TEST_User_Backend / test_backend@accessverse.org) and 1 consultation (TEST_Consult_User / test_consult@accessverse.org). UI tests created 1 quote (UI Test User) and 1 consultation (Consult UI Tester). All persisted in MongoDB collections quote_requests and consultations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend server is stable; PATCH routes use Body(...) as expected. Emergent Resend proxy occasionally logs errors but does NOT block form success responses in current testing. Dashboard is reached via the 'Compliance Tracker' nav tab (data-testid=nav-admin-dashboard-tab), NOT the /dashboard URL path. Status update uses a native HTML <select> element (no data-testid on select), values are 'Pending'/'In Remediation'/'Delivered' for quotes and 'Confirmed'/'Completed'/'Cancelled' for consultations (per Dashboard.jsx).
rca of the issue: Previous 502 was because two PATCH endpoints declared body as `payload: dict = Field(...)`, which Pydantic v2 rejected at import (Field must have proper default/kwargs). This crashed FastAPI startup, so all requests returned 502 at the ingress. Fix (already in place): switched to `payload: dict = Body(...)`. Verified via pytest — /api/ returns 200 and both PATCH endpoints return 200 with updated payload.
